title: Infinite Loop Detector
constraints: The agent must detect when it is repeating the same action and break out of the loop
The maximum iteration limit must be configurable
When the loop is broken, the agent must return a meaningful message about what it tried
The detection must work for both exact repeats and semantically similar repeated actions
hints: Track the history of actions taken and compare new actions against recent history
Set a hard iteration cap (e.g. 5) and exit the loop when reached
Compare the current tool call and arguments to the previous N calls to detect repetition

starterCode.langgraph:
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated
import operator

class State(TypedDict):
    query: str
    result: str
    actions: Annotated[list[str], operator.add]

def analyze(state: State) -> State:
    return {"actions": ["analyze"], "result": "Need more data"}

def fetch_data(state: State) -> State:
    return {"actions": ["fetch_data"], "result": "Data insufficient"}

def should_continue(state: State) -> str:
    # BUG: Always routes back to analyze — creates an infinite loop
    if state["result"] == "Done":
        return "end"
    return "analyze"

graph = StateGraph(State)
graph.add_node("analyze", analyze)
graph.add_node("fetch_data", fetch_data)
graph.add_edge(START, "analyze")
graph.add_edge("analyze", "fetch_data")
graph.add_conditional_edges("fetch_data", should_continue, {"analyze": "analyze", "end": END})

app = graph.compile()

# Test: This will loop forever
result = app.invoke({"query": "Analyze sales data", "result": "", "actions": []})
print(result["result"])
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai.tools import tool

@tool
def analyze_data(query: str) -> str:
    """Analyze the given data query."""
    return "Analysis incomplete — need to re-analyze with more context"

@tool
def fetch_more_data(query: str) -> str:
    """Fetch additional data for analysis."""
    return "Additional data fetched but still insufficient"

# BUG: Agent keeps calling tools in a loop with no exit condition
# TODO: Add loop detection and a maximum iteration limit
assistant = Agent(
    role="Data Analyst",
    goal="Analyze the data completely. Keep trying until you get a complete result.",
    backstory="You are a data analyst. You must keep analyzing until done.",
    tools=[analyze_data, fetch_more_data],
)

task = Task(
    description="Analyze the Q4 sales data",
    expected_output="A complete analysis of Q4 sales data",
    agent=assistant,
)

crew = Crew(agents=[assistant], tasks=[task])
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from agents.tool import function_tool

@function_tool
def analyze_data(query: str) -> str:
    """Analyze the given data query."""
    return "Analysis incomplete — need to re-analyze with more context"

@function_tool
def fetch_more_data(query: str) -> str:
    """Fetch additional data for analysis."""
    return "Additional data fetched but still insufficient"

# BUG: Agent keeps calling tools in a loop with no exit condition
# TODO: Add loop detection and a maximum iteration limit
agent = Agent(
    name="Data Analyst",
    instructions="You are a data analyst. Analyze the data completely. Keep trying until you get a complete result.",
    tools=[analyze_data, fetch_more_data],
)

result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "Analyze the Q4 sales data")
print(result.final_output)

evaluationCriteria.label: Infinite loop is detected
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent detects when it is stuck repeating the same actions

evaluationCriteria.label: Loop is broken after N iterations
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent exits the loop after a configurable maximum number of iterations

evaluationCriteria.label: Exit message is meaningful
evaluationCriteria.description: When breaking out, the agent reports what it tried and why it stopped

evaluationCriteria.label: Non-looping flows still work
evaluationCriteria.description: Queries that complete normally are not affected by the loop detection
